name: PM-KISAN (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi)
description: Direct income support of ₹6,000 per year to all landholding farmer families across the country, paid in three equal instalments of ₹2,000 every four months.
eligibility: All landholding farmer families with cultivable land. Excludes institutional landholders, former and current holders of constitutional posts, and income-tax payers.
benefits: ₹6,000 per year in 3 instalments of ₹2,000 each, transferred directly to the farmer's bank account via DBT.

name: PM Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY)
description: Comprehensive crop insurance scheme providing financial support to farmers suffering crop loss or damage due to unforeseen events like natural calamities, pests, and diseases.
eligibility: All farmers growing notified crops in notified areas, including sharecroppers and tenant farmers. Both loanee and non-loanee farmers are eligible.
benefits: Premium rates: 2% for Kharif, 1.5% for Rabi, and 5% for commercial/horticultural crops. Govt subsidises the remaining premium. Claims settled based on crop cutting experiments.

name: Soil Health Card Scheme
description: Provides every farmer a soil health card with crop-wise nutrient recommendations for their farm, promoting balanced fertilizer usage to improve productivity and soil health.
eligibility: All farmers across India. Local agriculture department collects soil samples and issues cards.
benefits: Free soil testing and a detailed Soil Health Card with nutrient status (N, P, K, S, micronutrients, pH) and fertilizer recommendations for 6 crop cycles.

name: Kisan Credit Card (KCC)
description: Provides short-term formal credit to farmers at subsidized interest rates for crop production, post-harvest expenses, and allied agricultural activities.
eligibility: All farmers — individual or joint borrowers, owner cultivators, tenant farmers, sharecroppers, and SHGs of farmers. Fisheries and animal husbandry farmers are also covered.
benefits: Credit limit based on landholding and crop pattern. Interest rate of 7% p.a. with 3% subvention for prompt repayment (effective rate: 4%). Insurance coverage under PMFBY. Credit up to ₹3 lakh without collateral.

name: e-NAM (National Agriculture Market)
description: A pan-India electronic trading portal linking existing APMC mandis to create a unified national market for agricultural commodities, giving farmers better price discovery.
eligibility: All farmers, traders, and commission agents registered with an APMC. Farmers need to register on the e-NAM portal with Aadhaar and bank details.
benefits: Access to buyers across state borders leading to better prices, transparent bidding, direct online payment to farmers, and reduced intermediary costs.

name: PM-KUSUM (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan)
ministry: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
description: Aims to solarize agriculture by providing subsidized solar pumps and enabling farmers to set up solar power plants on barren/fallow land to earn additional income.
eligibility: All farmers with agricultural land connection or diesel pumps. Component A: Farmers, cooperatives, panchayats with barren land. Component B & C: Individual farmers for standalone and grid-connected solar pumps.
benefits: Component A: Install solar plants of 500 kW to 2 MW on barren land. Component B: Standalone solar pumps of up to 7.5 HP with 60% subsidy. Component C: Solarise existing grid-connected pumps with 60% subsidy.

name: Rashtriya Krishi Vikas Yojana (RKVY-RAFTAAR)
description: Incentivises states to increase public investment in agriculture and allied sectors by providing flexibility and autonomy in planning and executing agriculture development projects.
eligibility: State governments apply for funds; benefits flow to individual farmers through state-level agricultural projects, FPOs, and agri-startups.
benefits: Funding for agri-infrastructure, innovation, value addition, and agri-entrepreneurship. Up to ₹25 lakh for individual agri-startup proposals.

name: National Horticulture Mission (NHM)
description: Promotes holistic growth of the horticulture sector through area-based regionally differentiated strategies covering fruits, vegetables, root and tuber crops, mushrooms, spices, flowers, aromatic plants, coconut, cashew, and cocoa.
eligibility: Farmers, FPOs, and private entrepreneurs engaged in horticulture. Different components have different eligibility criteria. Generally, small and marginal farmers get higher subsidy rates.
benefits: Subsidy of 40-75% on establishing new orchards, nurseries, protected cultivation (polyhouses, shade nets), micro-irrigation, post-harvest management, and cold chain infrastructure.

name: Micro Irrigation Fund (MIF)
ministry: Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare / NABARD
description: Dedicated fund to expand coverage of micro-irrigation (drip and sprinkler) across India, aiming to use water more efficiently and boost per-drop productivity for farmers.
eligibility: All farmers; state governments access the fund through NABARD and implement through agriculture/horticulture departments. Small and marginal farmers get priority.
benefits: Subsidy of 55% for small/marginal farmers and 45% for other farmers on drip and sprinkler irrigation systems. Also covers fertigation systems.

name: YSR Rythu Bharosa
description: Andhra Pradesh's investment support scheme providing financial assistance to farmers to meet their farming investment needs at the onset of every crop season.
eligibility: All farmer families in Andhra Pradesh owning cultivable land. Tenant farmers (Rythu Bharosa - PM KISAN) with RoFR certificates are also eligible.
benefits: ₹13,500 per year — ₹7,500 during Kharif (including ₹4,000 state + ₹2,000 PM-KISAN), ₹4,000 during Rabi, and ₹2,000 during summer via PM-KISAN.

name: Rythu Bandhu (Telangana)
description: Telangana's farmer investment support scheme providing direct financial assistance per acre of cultivable land to support agricultural input costs.
eligibility: All land-owning farmers in Telangana with pattadar passbooks. Each farmer gets investment support proportional to their landholding.
benefits: ₹10,000 per acre per year (₹5,000 for Kharif and ₹5,000 for Rabi) deposited directly into farmer's bank account.

name: Krushak Assistance for Livelihood and Income Augmentation (KALIA) — Odisha
description: Odisha's comprehensive farmer assistance scheme providing financial support for cultivation, livelihood support to landless agricultural households, and life insurance coverage.
eligibility: Small and marginal farmers, landless agricultural households, and vulnerable agricultural households in Odisha.
benefits: ₹25,000/- per farm family for crop cultivation over 5 seasons. ₹12,500/- per year for landless agricultural households. Life insurance cover of ₹2 lakh at ₹330/year premium.

name: Karnataka Raitha Siri
description: Karnataka's input subsidy scheme providing financial assistance for purchase of seeds, fertilizers, and other agricultural inputs to help farmers during crop season.
eligibility: All registered farmers in Karnataka holding agricultural land. Priority given to small and marginal farmers.
benefits: ₹10,000 per acre per year for irrigation farmers and ₹5,000 per acre for rain-fed farmers, transferred directly to the farmer's bank account.

name: Sub-Mission on Agricultural Mechanisation (SMAM)
description: Promotes farm mechanisation by providing subsidies on purchase of agricultural machinery and equipment and establishing Custom Hiring Centres and Farm Machinery Banks.
eligibility: Individual farmers, FPOs, cooperatives, SHGs, and entrepreneurs for Custom Hiring Centres. SC/ST/small/marginal farmers get higher subsidy.
benefits: Subsidy of 40-50% for individual farmers on tractors, power tillers, harvesters, threshers, and other machines. Up to 80% for Custom Hiring Centre establishment.

name: Paramparagat Krishi Vikas Yojana (PKVY)
description: Promotes organic farming through a cluster-based approach, supporting farmers in adopting organic practices and getting certification for selling produce at premium prices.
eligibility: Groups of 50 or more farmers forming a cluster of 20 hectares or more. Individual farmers can join through the cluster model. Priority to NE states and hilly areas.
benefits: ₹50,000 per hectare over 3 years — includes ₹31,000 for organic inputs, ₹8,800 for mobilization, ₹5,000 for PGS certification, and ₹5,200 for value addition and marketing.

name: Agriculture Infrastructure Fund (AIF)
description: A medium-to-long term financing facility for investment in agriculture infrastructure projects at farm-gate and aggregation points like cold stores, warehouses, packaging units, and processing plants.
eligibility: Farmers, FPOs, PACS, marketing cooperative societies, SHGs, joint liability groups, agri-entrepreneurs, and start-ups. All eligible entities can avail multiple loans.
benefits: Interest subvention of 3% per annum on loans up to ₹2 crore. Credit guarantee coverage up to ₹2 crore under CGTMSE. Loan tenure up to 7 years with moratorium.
